chunks.heading: What is Member Self-Service (MSS)?
chunks.text: The Fund's Member Self-Service (MSS) is a secure digital portal for managing your participation and pension with the Fund. Once registered, you can use it to submit pension forms, manage pension information, and run estimates.

chunks.heading: What can I do in MSS as an active participant?
chunks.text: As a participant, you can: check your personal information; run estimates of future pension benefits and options; access your annual pension statement; access UNJSPF forms pre-completed with your name and unique ID; track the Fund's receipt of separation documents; electronically submit required forms; and apply to validate, restore, or transfer pension rights.

chunks.heading: What can I do in MSS as a retiree or beneficiary?
chunks.text: As a retiree or beneficiary, you can: track all payments from the Fund and after-service health insurance premium deductions; access important pension documents; access the PDF of your annual Certificate of Entitlement (CE), unless paid under the two-track system; track whether your returned CE was received by the Fund; upload and submit signed pension forms and supporting documents; update your address and provide an emergency contact; submit an initial emergency fund request in case of severe financial hardship; and run an estimate of entitlement under the two-track option if you retired after 2 August 2015.

chunks.heading: How do I register for MSS and where can I find more information?
chunks.text: For more information and to register, visit unjspf.org.
